title: hieracium levicaule subsp. tephrellum
intro: Hieracium levicaule subsp. tephrellum, also known as hawkweed, is a flowering plant species within the dandelion-like genus Hieracium, native to certain regions of the world. This plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Hieracium levicaule subsp. tephrellum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin irritations, among others. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are typically made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
